reasoning: Medtronic (MDT) recently saw a drop from around $91 in early March to $84.93 by March 28, driven by missed sales estimates in its prior earnings and concerns over flat overall revenue growth. However, approximately $0.70 of the drop at the end of March was due to the stock trading ex-dividend on March 28. Because the benchmark price on March 30 is the post-dividend close of $84.93, it acts as an artificially lowered threshold. For very short horizons (April 6), the ongoing negative momentum counters some of the likely post-dividend rebound, keeping the probability close to 50%. Over longer horizons (1-12 months), standard equity risk premiums (long-term drift) and potential reversion to the mean gradually raise the probability that MDT will close above the current low point. Thus, the probabilities slowly scale upward mirroring standard random-walk-with-drift dynamics.
